name: Lutheran Social Service Of Minnesota
description_short: NFCC-certified nonprofit financial counseling service offering free and confidential debt management, budget counseling, and housing support for over 35 years.
description_long:
Lutheran Social Service of Minnesota (LSS) is an established nonprofit organization that has provided financial counseling services for more than 35 years. The organization is certified by the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) and operates as a trusted, nonjudgmental financial wellness provider serving Minnesota residents and organizations.

LSS Financial Counseling offers a comprehensive suite of free and confidential services designed to help consumers achieve financial wellness. Their core offerings include consumer credit counseling, credit improvement counseling, budget and debt management counseling, nonprofit Debt Management Plans, student loan debt repayment counseling, housing counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, and foreclosure prevention counseling. All services are provided by NFCC-certified financial counselors who deliver realistic, action-oriented, and confidential guidance.

What distinguishes LSS is their combination of long operational history, nonprofit status, and NFCC certification—indicators of regulatory compliance and professional standards. They emphasize nonjudgmental support and focus on developing healthy financial habits rather than simply addressing immediate debt crises. Their multilingual website (40+ languages) demonstrates commitment to serving diverse communities. The organization also offers Financial Choice, a customizable financial wellness service for employers, organizations, and groups seeking to provide employee financial education.

As a free nonprofit counseling service, LSS is best suited for consumers seeking genuinely no-cost financial guidance without sales pressure toward expensive products. The main limitation is geographic focus (Minnesota-based, though contact suggests broader service availability) and lack of details about response times, counselor availability, or specific debt management plan terms on their public website.
pros: NFCC-certified financial counselors ensure professional standards and regulatory compliance
All core services are completely free and confidential with no mandatory fees
Offers nonprofit Debt Management Plans as alternative to commercial debt settlement
Provides specialized student loan debt counseling addressing repayment options and default prevention
40+ language options on website demonstrates accessibility for immigrant and multilingual communities
Foreclosure prevention and housing counseling available, addressing critical housing stability needs
35+ years operational history demonstrates institutional stability and consumer trust
Tailored financial education programs available for employers and organizations through Financial Choice
cons: Website lacks specific information on average counseling session duration, response times, or availability windows
No details provided about Debt Management Plan terms, monthly fees (if any), or creditor participation rates
Geographic focus unclear—Minnesota-based but extent of service beyond Minnesota not specified on financial counseling pages
Limited online account management or digital tools described; appears primarily phone and in-person based
No pricing transparency for organizational Financial Choice service; requires direct contact for quotes
best_for: Consumers in Minnesota seeking free, nonprofit debt counseling without pressure to purchase additional products
Individuals facing foreclosure or housing instability needing specialized homeownership and prevention counseling
Federal student loan borrowers confused about repayment options and seeking guidance on avoiding default
Non-English speakers and immigrant communities given extensive multilingual support (40+ languages)
diagnosis: LSS Financial Counseling is ideal for Minnesota residents and broader consumers seeking genuinely free, nonprofit financial counseling from certified professionals without sales pressure or hidden fees. The primary caveat is that the website provides minimal operational details about response times, availability, and specific plan terms—direct contact via 888.577.2227 is necessary for concrete service expectations.
services: Financial wellness education for organizations (Financial Choice program)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 716 E Street, East Brainerd Mall, Brainerd, MN 56401
